title: The Midnight Email Era
content: I used to answer emails at midnight. Every night. Then I had a boss who said: 'If you're always available, you're never valuable.' Changed how I work. Boundaries aren't weakness - they're strategy.
extended_content: Gloria Martinez again. She noticed I was responding to her late-night emails within minutes. 'Alex,' she said, 'stop that. When you answer at midnight, you train people to expect midnight answers. And then you burn out. Protect your time, or no one else will.' It felt wrong at first, like I was letting people down. But she was right. My best work happens when I'm rested. My worst decisions happen at midnight. Now 5:30 PM means done. Unless it's a genuine emergency - and genuine emergencies are rarer than people think.
when_to_tell: when discussing work-life balance or boundaries
lesson: Boundaries protect your ability to do your best work
